etymology.description: 肉 (meat) + 又 (hand) = originally related forms -> current interpretation as ancient form of 有 (yǒu, 'to have')
etymology.interpretation: Originally, 㞢 depicted a combination of a hand and meat, suggesting possession or holding. However, it is now historically considered an ancient variation of 有 (yǒu, 'to have'), which was further modified with other forms for clarity.
